commit.message:
Complete replication with ITT extension, add figures, update CI

- Add Bloomberg float-adjusted rankings, fuzzy 2SLS, and reduced-form
  (ITT) estimators for the 1996-2024 Russell RD replication
- Add robustness checks: sharp vs fuzzy, donut hole, BH FDR, IO-tercile
  heterogeneity, clustered SEs, placebo cutoffs, pre-trend validity
- Extend analysis to 2015-2024 using ITT (2SLS underpowered post-banding)
- Rewrite README with full results, methodology, and figures
- Track output figures in files/ (previously gitignored)
- Remove dev artifacts from tracking (CLAUDE.md, AGENT_SUMMARY.md, etc.)
- Update CI to run tests + ruff lint only (notebook requires proprietary data)
- Fix all ruff lint errors across auxiliary/ and tests/
- 44 tests passing, ruff clean

commit.message:
fix: run ALTER TABLE unconditionally, not just as migration fallback

Prisma marks the migration as applied even when DDL fails, so
migrate deploy returns success while the column is still missing.
Run ALTER TABLE IF NOT EXISTS after every deploy to guarantee it.

commit.message:
fix: add warn logging to silent catch blocks in websocket poll loops (#847)

Add console.warn logging to the UI poll and session poll catch
blocks which previously swallowed all non-AbortError exceptions
without any indication of what failed.

- UI poll: logs "[UI poll] Connection error (retry N): <message>"
- Session poll: logs "[Session poll] Connection error for session
  <id> (retry N): <message>" with session context

Both loops already handled AbortError silently (expected: visibility
resume or stop). Non-abort errors are unexpected connection failures
that warrant WARN-level visibility for debugging.

Fixes #835

commit.message:
Fix deferred Connect: create event loop for background thread

ib_insync requires an asyncio event loop on the calling thread.
When _run_ibkr_connection runs on a background thread (from the
Connect button), there's no event loop — causing
'There is no current event loop in thread' error.

Fix: create and set a new event loop at the start of
_run_ibkr_connection before calling ib.connect().

commit.message:
refactor: extract Docker /tmp helpers into docker_utils.py (#844)

Centralize duplicated /tmp path-translation and session tmp-dir
management that was scattered across three modules:

- Add `translate_docker_tmp_path` (async): translates /tmp/… paths to
  session-scoped host paths for Docker sessions; no-ops for non-/tmp
  paths, non-Docker sessions, missing session info, or on error.
- Add `get_session_tmp_dir`: returns `session_dir / "tmp"`.
- Add `cleanup_session_tmp`: removes the session tmp dir on session end,
  logs exceptions instead of raising.

Call sites updated:
- src/mcp/resource_mcp_tools.py: 13-line block → 2-line call
- src/legion/mcp/legion_mcp_tools.py: 11-line block → 2-line call
- src/session_coordinator.py: inline tmp creation + 8-line cleanup
  block each replaced with single-line helper calls

Add src/tests/test_docker_utils.py with 11 unit tests covering all
three helpers (happy paths, no-ops, error handling).

Fixes #832

commit.message:
fix: remove stale sessionCursor from session poll loop (#843)

Replace module-level sessionCursor variable with direct reads from
the per-session sessionCursors cache in the connectSession() poll loop.

The module-level variable was shared across all concurrent poll loops.
Rapid session switching caused it to be overwritten by the most recent
connectSession() call, making in-flight loops use a stale cursor and
either replay history or skip messages.

- Remove `let sessionCursor = 0` declaration
- Remove `sessionCursor = sessionCursors[sessionId]` local copy
- Update poll URL to read `sessionCursors[sessionId]` directly
- Remove `sessionCursor = data.next_cursor` redundant update

sessionCursors[sessionId] is now the sole source of truth.

Fixes #831

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

feat: close final parity gaps — local reactions, Coulomb helper, time modulation

Three remaining Python-Rust gaps resolved:

1. reaction_to_local() — transforms global-frame joint reactions to
   body-local frame via inverse rotation. extract_reactions_with_local()
   populates force_local_i/j on JointReaction.

2. ForceElement::coulomb_friction() — convenience constructor for pure
   Coulomb friction (subset of BearingFriction with zero drag/viscous).

3. TimeModulation enum — external forces/torques can now vary with time
   via Constant, Sinusoidal, Step, or Ramp modulation. Serializable and
   backward-compatible (defaults to Constant).

Full Python feature parity achieved. 374 tests pass.

commit.message:
fix: remove sessionPolling/uiPolling flags, use reactive refs as sole loop-control (#842)

Replace the module-level `sessionPolling` and `uiPolling` booleans with
the existing reactive refs (`sessionConnected`, `uiConnected`) as the
sole loop-control signals. This eliminates the possibility of the flag
and reactive ref diverging.

Key changes:
- UI poll: add `uiPollGeneration` counter; `stopUIPolling()` increments
  it; catch block re-asserts `uiConnected=true` only when generation
  matches, guarding against stale re-assertion during stop
- Session poll: `disconnectSession()` nulls `currentSessionId`; catch
  block re-asserts `sessionConnected=true` only when
  `currentSessionId.value === sessionId`, same guard pattern
- Remove redundant `sessionConnected.value = true` inside try block
  (now set before loop and re-asserted after retry)

No behavior changes. External consumers of `sessionConnected` are all
read-only and unaffected.

Fixes #833

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
fix: globe grid now renders as a recognizable sphere, not garbled lines

Root cause: sphere was 90% of screen size with center at 75%/85% off-screen,
so the viewport only showed a tiny warped corner with extreme perspective
distortion — looked like random intersecting lines.

Fix:
- Radius now 40% of min(w,h) — entire sphere visible
- Center at 70%/65% — globe sits in bottom-right but is mostly visible
- Perspective distance scales proportionally (3x radius) — clean projection
- Added X-axis tilt (0.3 rad) for a natural viewing angle
- Bumped default opacity to 0.14 for better visibility
- Size slider now controls actual visible globe size

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Restructure categories: all instruments from MOEX only

New categories:
1. Криптовалюты (Bybit) - BTC, ETH, SOL, BNB, XRP
2. Акции РФ (MOEX TQBR) - SBER, GAZP, LKOH, YDEX, ROSN
3. Товарные фьючерсы (MOEX FORTS) - Gold, Silver, Platinum,
   Palladium, Copper, Brent, Gas, Wheat, Sugar, Cocoa, Si, Eu, CR
4. Фьючерсы на индексы (MOEX FORTS) - IMOEX, RTS, NASDAQ, S&P500,
   Bitcoin futures
5. Облигации (MOEX TQOB) - OFZ 26238, 26243, 26240

Removed: "Акции США" category (was mix of currency/US index futures)
Added futures prefixes: SUGAR=SA, CU=CU, ALUM=AL, NICK=NL, RTS=RI, BTCF=BA
All dataTicker now point to MOEX futures base codes (proxy auto-resolves)

commit.message:
fix: align OTP screen buttons with Figma design (#3588) (#3591)

* fix: align OTP screen buttons with Figma design (#3588)

- Paste button: remove fillMaxHeight, add border, fix padding (17/12dp → 20/16dp)
- "Use a different email": CircleShape → RoundedCornerShape(12dp), fix background
  color (ctaDisabled → backgrounds.tertiary_2), text color (secondary → button.primary),
  text style (body.s.medium → supplementary.footnote)
- Make inner FastVaultVerificationScreen composable internal for PreviewActivity

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct VsCodeInputField box sizing so Paste button fits (#3588)

The extra paddingPx (24dp) added on top of the already-measured text size
(which included its own 12dp padding) caused each box to be ~90dp wide,
making 4 boxes total ~360dp — leaving no horizontal room for the Paste button
and causing its text to wrap. Removing the redundant paddingPx and the
measurement-only text padding lets the sizing hit the 46dp floor, producing
46×58dp boxes that match the Figma design and leave space for Paste.

- Change corner radius from CircleShape to RoundedCornerShape(24.dp)
- Fix inter-cell gap from 8dp to 6dp
- Remove weight() modifiers that conflicted with fixed cell dimensions
- Fix Paste button to fixed 78x46dp with correct padding

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

perf: remove wasteful skip-history poll on session connect (#839)

Replace the throwaway timeout=0 HTTP request used to advance the
long-poll cursor with a simple cursor initialisation to 0.

The skip-poll was added to avoid replaying historical events, but
loadMessages() already fetches full message history via REST and the
message store deduplicates by ID, so cursor-0 replay produces no
duplicate UI entries. The extra round-trip on every session connection
was pure waste.

Reconnect behaviour is unchanged: when a cached cursor already exists
(session switch or re-connect) the cached value is used as before.

Fixes #829

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Builder-793 <builder@claudecode.webui>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: replace O(n) EventQueue.events_since() scan with O(1) index lookup (#838)

Replace deque[tuple[int, dict]] with list[dict] + _oldest_cursor tracking.
Since cursors are sequential integers, the first relevant index is computable
in O(1): start_idx = max(0, cursor - _oldest_cursor + 1).

- Remove deque import (no longer used)
- Add _oldest_cursor: int = 1 field to track eviction offset
- events_since() now slices instead of scanning all events
- append() uses list.pop(0) + _oldest_cursor increment for eviction

All 27 existing EventQueue and long-poll tests pass unchanged.

Fixes #828

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Builder-793 <builder@claudecode.webui>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: globe grid visible again — use depth-based alpha instead of hard backface cull

Previous approach (z3d > 0 = hide) was too aggressive and hid the entire
visible portion since the sphere is offset to bottom-right. Now uses smooth
depth-based alpha: front-facing lines are bright, back-facing lines are
very faint (15% of front alpha). No hard culling — all lines render but
with proper depth perception.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

ORH_FBO_V2_B expansion: PM continuation + premium late-day sleeves

Added two new ORH_B-specific admission paths inside _try_signal():

Primary PM continuation (hour>=12):
  body_fraction>=0.60, counter_wick<=0.30, bar_return<=-0.20
  Forces A-tier to bypass generic quality gate

Premium late-day (hour>=13):
  body_fraction>=0.55, bar_return<=-0.15, rel_impulse<=-0.10
  Forces A-tier to bypass generic quality gate

Results:
  Before: N=14, PF=1.93, +5.9R
  After:  N=21, PF=3.47, +17.4R (+11.5R added)
  PM sleeve: N=13, +19.6R, WR=85%
  Monthly distribution: spread across 9 months, no concentration

Portfolio: N=183, PF=2.21, +71.7R (was N=176, PF=2.03, +60.2R)

Metadata now includes body_fraction, counter_wick_fraction, bar_return_pct,
relative_impulse_vs_spy, and sleeve flags for attribution.
